findings.title: Consolidate hidden default-app selection path
findings.suggested_fix: Move default-app association selection fully under macos_preferences.default_apps for new selections, and handle legacy dock.defaults with an explicit one-way compatibility adapter that de-duplicates categories before calling backup_macos_preferences. Remove DOCK_DEFAULTS from the wizard state/summary once legacy cache migration is isolated.

findings.title: Unify macOS preference category metadata
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the separate category label array, domains case statement, and backup special cases with one category registry that records id, label, kind (defaults/report/default_apps), and handler data. Derive wizard labels, recommended selections, validation, and backup dispatch from that registry so adding or renaming a category is a single-site change.

residual_risks: Review was limited to maintainability concerns in the scoped diff; correctness and security risks in preference import/export semantics are left to their dedicated reviewers.
The new macOS preference categories touch OS-specific behavior that can drift between macOS releases, so registry design should leave room for version-gated categories or handlers.
testing_gaps: No tests appear to cover legacy selections containing dock.defaults together with macos_preferences.default_apps, so duplicate category handling and migration behavior are unverified.
The regression test exercises a few categories but not the category registry itself; a lightweight test that every advertised category has a dispatch path would catch future no-op category additions.
